rulings.Case Title: JANE MELC et al VS. PATRICK DOHERTY et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, July 28, 2011, line 3, DEFENDANTS PATRICK DOHERTY, DOHERTY CONSTRUCTION, DOHERTY CONSTRUCTION & DOYLE DEVELOPMENT MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. DEFENDANTS MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT/ADJUDICATION IS GRANTED AS TO THE 1ST, 2ND AND 9TH CAUSES OF ACTION, THE PLAINTIFF IS ATTEMPTING TO DO WHAT IS EXPRESSLY PROHIBITED UNDER THE SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT. MOREOVER, PLAINTIFFS ARGUMENT THAT THERE IS A BASIS FOR RECISSION OF THE SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT IS NOT CONVINCING. INFORMATION REGARDING LICENSING OF DEFENDANTS IS A MATTER OF PUBLIC RECORD WHICH WAS INFORMATION EASILY OBTAINABLE BY PLANTIFFS AT THE TIME THE SETTLEMENT WAS ENTERED INTO. CARROLL V. DUNGEY (1966) 223 CAL.APP.2D 247, 255. AS TO THE 3RD CAUSE OF ACTION, THE COURT FINDS THAT DEFENDANTS WERE NOT PARTIES TO THE CONTRACT REFERENCED IN THAT CAUSE OF ACTION. BECAUSE THE PLEADINGS MUST DETERMINE THE SCOPE OF RELEVANT ISSUES ON A MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, THE COURT CANNOT CONSIDER PLAINTIFFS COUNSEL'S PLEA OF "OVERSIGHT" OR "MISTAKE". NIETO V. BLUE SHIELD OF CAL. LIFE & HEALTH INS. CO. (2010) 181 CAL.APP.4TH 60, 73. PLAINTIFFS REQUEST FOR A CONTINUANCE TO CONDUCT THE DEPOSITION OF DEFENDANT DOYLE IS DENIED. FINALLY PLAINTIFFS OBJECTIONS WILL NOT BE CONSIDERED AS THEY WERE NOT PROPERLY FILED WITH THE OPPOSITION BRIEF AS REQUIRED UNDER CRC 3.1354(a). =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: JOSHUA RUXIN VS. CLUB MED SALES, INC. et al

rulings.Case Title: JOWONNA BUTLER et al VS. EDGAR GARCIA et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, July 28, 2011, line 4, DEFENDANTS EDGAR GARCIA, JANE CREAGER MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. The Defendants Special Motion to Strike (Anti-SLAPP) the 1st, 2nd, 3rd and 4th causes of action is granted. Defendant has met its burden under the first prong of the §425.16 analysis by showing that the acts constituting the gravamen of these claims primarily arises from protected free speech or petitioning activity. Haight Ashbury Free Clinics Inc. v. Happening Houses Ventures, (2010) 184 Cal.App.4th 1539. Having done so, the burden then passes to Plaintiff on the second prong of the analysis to prove that it will likely prevail on the merits. Plaintiff fails to present any admissible evidence demonstrating that they have a probability of prevailing. Accordingly, the Court strikes the 1st through 4th causes of action. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: JEWISH COMMUNITY RELATIONS COUNCIL OF SAN et al VS. JOHN ARNTZ, IN HIS OFFICIAL CAPACITY AS DIRECTOR et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Grant Writ Of Mandate And Injuinctive Relief; Compilation
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, July 28, 2011, line 8, PETITIONERS JEWISH COMMUNITY RELATIONS COUNCIL OF SAN FRANCISCO, THE PENINSULA, MARIN, SONOMA, ALAMEDA AND CONTRA COSTA COUNTIES, THE ANTI-DEFAMATION LEAGUE, JEREMY BENJAMIN, JENNY BENJAMIN, LEO FUCHS, JONATHAN JAFFE, YAEL FRENKEL-JAFFE, SHEILA BARI, LETICIA PREZA, KASHIF ABDULLAH, BRIAN MCBETH, ERIC TABAS, Motion To Grant Writ Of Mandate And Injunctive Relief. The Court finds that the proposed ballot Initiative is expressly preempted by California Business and Professions §460(b). The evidence presented is overwhelmingly persuasive that circumcision is a widely practiced medical procedure. California Business and Professions Code §460 (b) applies to medical services provided by a wide range of health care professionals. The statute speaks directly to the issue of local regulation of medical procedures and leaves no room for localities to regulate in this area. In fact, the legislative history of §460(b) confirms that the legislature intended to prevent cities and counties from regulating medical services which is a matter statewide concern. Because the proposed ballot initiative attempts to regulate a medical procedure, the proposed ordinance is expressly preempted. Moreover, it serves no legitimate purpose to allow a measure whose invalidity can be determined as a matter of law to remain on the ballot after such a ruling has been made. City of San Diego v. Dunkl, (2001) 86 Cal.App.4th 384, 389 Accordingly, the Court issues a Writ of Mandate Ordering the Director of Elections for the City and County of San Francisco to remove the measure from the ballot in its entirety. The applications to file a brief as amicus curiae, to file a brief in excess of the maximum number of pages, and for pro hac vice admission by the Doctors Opposing Circumcision are denied. These motions were filed two days before hearing and courtesy copies were not provided until the day before hearing, which is extremely untimely.

Set for hearing on Thursday, July 28, 2011, line 9, DEFENDANT SANDRA IBARRA'S MOTION FOR SET ASIDE/VACATE JUDGMENT. HEARING REQUIRED. MOVING PARTY TO APPEAR AND PROVIDE COURTESY COPY OF MOVING PAPERS. =(302/LMG)
